title: Windows 7, just curious
content: I installed Windows 7 just to satisfy my inner-geek's curiosity. Since I've already read [review](http://blogs.zdnet.com/hardware/?p=3223) after [review](http://news.cnet.com/windows-7-beta-first-impressions/) and even more [review](http://www.winsupersite.com/win7/win7_beta.asp) about "what's new" in this version, I didn't take much time playing with the system. And since I'm such an ego-centric person, I ended up opening my website on the Windows 7 version of IE. What is that? My perfectly-fine-on-IE6 website is completely messed up on Windows 7?!? Another reason for me to ignore IE. :P (I really **played** with the thing and so quickly delete it from the system, I didn't even get a chance to look at the version of IE. And good thing I have [VMware](http://vmware.com/). Heh.)
